channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How to watch New Zealand v England T20 series 2025 for FREE: live streams, schedule, teams
item.description: We'll show you all the ways you can watch New Zealand v England T20 live streams from anywhere in the world and potentially for free.
item.dc:creator: Roderick Easdale, TechRadar

item.title: Claude just got a skill upgrade and it’s coming for your workflows
item.description: Model builders are moving beyond simple AI chatbots to creating comprehensive assistants that, in the words of AI dignitary Ethan Mollick, “do real work” in enterprise workflows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Hi-fi legend Quad's new super-powerful, super-connected stereo amp could be all the amp you need for the rest of your life
item.description: Money No Object: Quad launches its "finest-ever" integrated amp, and it's future-proofed in a ton of ways
item.dc:creator: Carrie Marshall, TechRadar

item.title: Maine Senate candidate faces more resurfaced Reddit posts
item.description: Reports have found additional controversial social media posts Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner made years before his bid to oust Sen. Susan Collins (R).
item.dc:creator: Surina Venkat, The Hill

item.title: I helped build the internet’s ad economy. Now I want to save it
item.description: If you’ve noticed that the internet feels different lately—more cluttered, harder to navigate—you’re not imagining it. The system is breaking down in real time, and by 2026, researchers predict that 90% of web content will be AI-generated.
item.dc:creator: Brian O'Kelley, Fast Company

item.title: Apple’s Big Bang AI moment is approaching
item.description: Want to use artificial intelligence (AI) that maintains privacy and doesn’t need a lake full of water or industrial electricity generator to make an emojii? Apple has that.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australia sharing tips on curbing social media for children before age limit starts in December
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — The Australian government has begun a public education campaign with tips on how to wean children off social media ahead of a world-first national 16-year age limit taking effect in December.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sora is blocking people from nabbing celebrity and public figure usernames
item.description: As OpenAI rolls out its new social media app Sora—which allows users to prompt the company’s Sora 2 model to produce fantastical videos of almost anything—there are obvious concerns that the platform could be used to generate deepfakes and otherwise misleading content.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Heilweil, Fast Company

item.title: Big Tech is paying millions to train teachers on AI, in a push to bring chatbots into classrooms
item.description: The country’s largest teachers unions are teaming up with tech companies like Microsoft and OpenAI to help educators build literacy in artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Gecker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A mini-CrowdStrike moment? Windows 11 update cripples dev environments
item.description: Just as Windows 10 has reached its end of life (EOL), issues with Windows 11 abound, the most recent an update that broke localhost for some developers, preventing them from accessing web apps running locally on their machines.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Uber wants to become the ‘Uber’ of data labeling
item.description: Following its entrance into the data labeling space last year with the launch of a new division, Scaled Solutions, Uber on Thursday introduced a pilot project in the US market that will see its drivers, like their counterparts in India, annotate data for use in AI training.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Someone designed an open portable desk with a canvas handle to carry your $10,000 Apple MacBook Pro laptop around - but somehow I don't think it's a very good idea
item.description: The AeroTrac workstation reimagines portable setups for laptops, though its practicality for protecting costly devices remains questionable.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Chamber of Commerce sues to block $100K H-1B visa fees
item.description: The U.S. Chamber of Commerce sued the Trump administration Thursday over its decision to raise the fee for H-1B work visa applications to $100,000, arguing that President Trump exceeded his authority in altering the visa program.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Unions sue Trump administration over social media vetting of visa holders
item.description: Three labor unions sued the Trump administration Thursday in connection with a program that searched social media posts of visa holders, arguing that it violates the First Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Chipmaker TSMC reports nearly 40% surge in its net profit, thanks to AI
item.description: Taiwan’s leading computer chip maker, TSMC, said Thursday that its net profit surged nearly 40% in the last quarter, boosted by the surge in use of artificial intelligence. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Corp. is the world’s biggest semiconductor manufacturer.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Inside Microsoft’s quest to make Windows 11’s AI irresistible
item.description: People remember many things about Windows 95, which turned 30 a couple of months ago. There were its signature new features, such as the Start Button, taskbar, and long file names. The launch event—hosted by Jay Leno—at Microsoft’s campus. The TV commercials with the Rolling Stones’ “Start Me Up.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: As Broadcom becomes its latest major ally, this graph shows how OpenAI made itself too big to fail by securing hundreds of billions from the world's largest tech companies
item.description: Broadcom joins Nvidia, AMD, and Microsoft in OpenAI’s trillion-dollar AI infrastructure collaboration revealed in social media image.
item.dc:creator: Wayne Williams, TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft: Russia, China increasingly using AI to escalate cyberattacks on the US
item.description: Russia, China, Iran and North Korea are using artificial intelligence to improve their cyberattacks on U.S. companies, governments and individuals, according to new research from Microsoft.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Death of kidnapped South Korean student spurs talks with Cambodia to tackle online scams
item.description: PHNOM PENH, Cambodia (AP) — Cambodian and South Korean officials agreed Thursday to cooperate in combating online scams, after the death of a South Korean student who was reportedly forced to work in a scam center in Cambodia triggered public outrage in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and SOPHENG CHEANG, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Nvidia’s DGX Spark desktop supercomputer is on sale now, but hard to find
item.description: Nvidia’s “personal AI supercomputer,” the DGX Spark, may run fast but it’s been slow getting here. It finally went on sale on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Zoom dooms the developer’s afternoon
item.description: If you’re a software developer and you don’t already know of Paul Graham and his wonderful essays, then you’re in for a treat. Graham writes about programming, running startups, and, well, life.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Major network vendors team to advance Ethernet for scale-up AI networking
item.description: As AI networking technology blossoms, yet another group has formed to make sure Ethernet can handle the stress.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Army secretary tees up acquisition reforms amid ‘unprecedented’ top cover from Trump administration
item.description: Army Secretary Dan Driscoll said he will unveil changes that will "completely disrupt" the service's acquisition processes in the coming weeks. The post Army secretary tees up acquisition reforms amid ‘unprecedented’ top cover from Trump administration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: SpaceX launches the 11th test flight of its mega Starship rocket with another win
item.description: SpaceX launched another of its mammoth Starship rockets on a test flight Monday, successfully making it halfway around the world while releasing mock satellites like last time. Starship — the biggest and most powerful rocket ever built — thundered into the evening sky from the southern tip of Texas.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: OpenAI partners with Broadcom to design its own AI chips
item.description: OpenAI said Monday it is working with chipmaker Broadcom to design its own artificial intelligence computer chips. The two California companies didn't disclose the financial terms of the deal but said they will start deploying the new racks of customized “AI accelerators” late next year.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2025: Federal, industry IT leaders on how agencies are preparing for post-quantum cybersecurity
item.description: Air Force, CISA, NIST share how they are preparing for a future when quantum computers could break today’s encryption. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange 2025: Federal, industry IT leaders on how agencies are preparing for post-quantum cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Crypto spent millions to defeat Sherrod Brown and elect allies. It's ready for a repeat in 2026
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — At a five-star resort tucked in the mountains of Jackson Hole, Wyoming, the cryptocurrency industry was celebrating a historic start to the year on Capitol Hill. Its priorities were sailing through Congress with unusual speed and one senator did not hesitate to say why. Sen.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KREX 5
